arguments.task: Open the official Squarespace developer site and extract evidence for: whether there is a public API, auth methods, rate limits, pricing or access gating, commerce/ecommerce related APIs, and any official mentions of developer platform availability or deprecation. Then check official Squarespace support/pricing pages if needed. Also find any official docs page that mentions GraphQL or REST. Return the exact supporting URLs and concise quotes.

arguments.task: Using official Squarespace pages only, extract exact concise findings with URLs for these items: 1) Commerce APIs are REST and available for custom applications and Extensions; 2) auth methods including personal access tokens, OAuth, and plan requirements for commerce advanced/advanced plan; 3) exact rate limits and any headers/429 behavior; 4) whether there is any official GraphQL API mention for developer APIs; 5) any official statement that Developer Platform/Template docs are not supported on version 7.1. Return a compact bullet list with URLs.

input.thought: Run a narrower extraction task to get final precise facts and URLs from official pages.

raw_result.description: Website and ecommerce platform with developer-facing Commerce APIs for store data and order workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Commerce API docs describe personal access tokens for custom applications and OAuth for Squarespace Extensions/commercial development; docs also describe permission scopes. Some broader platform areas may have separate auth patterns, so auth coverage beyond the documented Commerce APIs should be human-checked.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official docs state Commerce APIs require a Commerce Advanced plan, and the pricing page indicates Commerce APIs are available on the Advanced plan.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer surface is centered on Commerce APIs with endpoints for commerce resources plus authentication, permissions, webhooks, requests, and rate limits; no official GraphQL developer API evidence was verified in the gathered pages.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Squarespace MCP server or registry evidence was verified from the gathered official pages; human review recommended for third-party MCP listings.

raw_result.blocker: A first toolkit appears buildable against the documented REST Commerce APIs, but access is gated to a paid commerce plan and evidence gathered is concentrated on Commerce APIs rather than the full product surface.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official developer docs include a dedicated rate-limits page and making-requests guidance, indicating documented rate limiting and limit-exceeded behavior; exact numeric limits were not reliably captured in the gathered evidence and should be human-verified from the rate-limits page.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer hub is live; Commerce APIs have dedicated docs for overview, auth, requests, and rate limits. Gathered evidence also indicated the template/developer platform area routes through Template Docs/quick start, with some navigation ambiguity and possible version/platform limitations that warrant human review.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Exact numeric rate limits were not captured in the gathered evidence, no official MCP evidence was verified, and navigation around Developer Platform/Template Docs was ambiguous, including possible version 7.1 support limitations and lack of explicit GraphQL confirmation.
